topic.name: Naval History of Japan
topic.description: The documents discuss various ships of the Imperial Japanese Navy, highlighting their historical significance and roles in naval warfare.

Document 1: Zuihō was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II.
Zuihō participated in the Philippine Sea and Leyte Gulf in mid-1944.
Document 2: Mikasa served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War.
Mikasa is the last remaining example of a pre-dreadnought battleship anywhere in the world.
Document 3: Libia was a protected cruiser built in Italy in the 1900s.
Libia had a relatively uneventful career.

topic.name: Infrastructure Development History
topic.description: The documents discuss the historical development and significance of various infrastructure projects, including buildings and roadways.

Document 1: The Wells Fargo Building , originally the Fidelity-Philadelphia Trust Company Building , is a skyscraper in Center City , Philadelphia , Pennsylvania
The building was long regarded as premier office space , but by the 1980s , tenants were leaving for newer buildings in the West Market Street neighborhood.
Document 2: The Causeway is an arterial traffic crossing in Perth , Western Australia , linking the inner-city suburbs of East Perth and Victoria Park
The project was first planned in 1834 and opened in 1843.
Document 3: Delaware Route 10 ( DE 10 ) is a state highway in Kent County , Delaware
What is now DE 10 between the Maryland border and Camden was constructed as a state highway in stages in the 1920s and early 1930s.

topic.name: Fungi and Mycology
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their taxonomy, morphology, and ecological roles.

Document 1: Collybia tuberosa, commonly known as the lentil shanklet or the appleseed coincap, is an inedible species of fungus in the Tricholomataceae family.
The fungus produces small whitish fruit bodies with caps up to 1 cm wide held by thin stems.
Document 2: Mycena sanguinolenta, commonly known as the bleeding bonnet, is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
The fungus produces reddish-brown to reddish-purple fruit bodies with conic to bell-shaped caps up to 1.5 cm wide.

topic.name: Mathematical Functions and Series
topic.description: This topic encompasses various mathematical concepts related to functions, including matrices, the Dirac delta function, and Taylor series, highlighting their definitions and applications.

Document 1: a matrix (plural matrices) is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations
Document 2: the Dirac delta function, or δ function, is a generalized function
the delta function only makes sense as a mathematical object when it appears inside an integral
Document 3: a Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms
A function can be approximated by using a finite number of terms of its Taylor series

Document 3: Codex Boreelianus is a 9th (or 10th) century uncial manuscript of the four Gospels in Greek.
The manuscript was named Boreelianus after Johannes Boreel.

topic.name: U.S. Constitutional Law and History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the principles, historical figures, and legal frameworks that shaped the United States Constitution and its interpretation, particularly in relation to criminal procedure and governance.

topic.name: Reformation and Church Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss the influence of the Reformation on theological beliefs and church architecture in Europe, particularly focusing on the works of Huldrych Zwingli and the architectural evolution in Scotland and Bulgaria.

Document 1: Zwingli 's views on baptism were largely a response to Anabaptism
He developed the symbolic view of the Eucharist.
Document 2: the Reformation revolutionised church architecture in Scotland

Document 3: The Church of Christ Pantocrator is usually dated to the late 13th or early 14th century.
